senses.gloss: to rise, to leap upwards, to spring

examples.text:
a. 1059, by Abū al-ʿAlāʾ Maʿarri
وَلَيْسَ الطِمِرُّ سِوَى مَا طَمَر
wa-laysa ṭ-ṭimirru siwā mā ṭamar
examples.english: There is nothing swift that does not leap.

senses.gloss: to become inflated, to become swollen, to intumesce
to bury, to landfill, to embed, to inter, to slam
to fold up, to roll up
to lock up, to put into the silo (مَطْمُورَة (maṭmūra))

examples.text:
a. 1059, by Abū al-ʿAlāʾ Maʿarri
وَلَيْسَ الطِمِرُّ سِوَى مَا طَمَر
wa-laysa ṭ-ṭimirru siwā mā ṭamar
examples.english: There is nothing swift that does not leap.
